title: Reviews: San Francisco Bay and Delta Model Bay Model Visitor Center | Discover Magazine
content: # Reviews: San Francisco Bay and Delta Model Bay Model Visitor Center. Discover reviews the San Francisco Bay and Delta Model Bay Model Visitor Center. These ancient portraits of what may have been the largest lions that ever existed, *Panthera spelaea,* tell us much about an extinct species, and even more about our own. As David Quammen explains in *Monster of God,* we have turned the tables on the world's great predators and are rapidly killing them off—despite, or ironically, because of—the powerful role that large carnivores have played in the human imagination. Eradicate the predators and we face what one expert calls ecological meltdown: a cascade of extinctions and environmental collapse—a world, as Quammen puts it, overrun by a "pestilence of nibblers, cropping the vegetation down to stubs." A similar catastrophe awaits the human spirit, he argues, if we eradicate the world's last man-eating beasts. In our prayers as well as in our nightmares, they remind us that we are not lords of the world.

title:
31K views · 694 reactions | While open as a federal penitentiary, Alcatraz Island always had an aura of mystery. Today, millions of visitors come to visit the island every year... but what are they coming to see? From soaking in the views of the San Francisco Bay, to watching a ranger-led cell door demonstration, step into the shoes of an Alcatraz visitor to see what it’s really like to visit The Rock! 

This short video includes different activities and sights on Alcatraz Island from a visitor’s perspective.  The video features short clips of the Alcatraz City Cruises ferry to the island and an NPS Park Ranger greeting visitors, the West Road and views of the San Francisco Bay, exhibits in the New Industries Building, an NPS Park Ranger opening and closing cell doors, and shots of Snowy Egret on Alcatraz. 

We look forward to seeing you on the island! 🛥️🌉

Music By: QualityBeats/Jamendo | Alcatraz Island

We look forward to seeing you on the island! ## **Alcatraz Island**. It’s nesting bird season here on Alcatraz and we’re seeing a change in the Brandt’s Cormorant population. While there are far fewer nests on Alcatraz than in 2025, we have chicks on the island!
